D10 Imaging L5 Evaluate Reference

RAW → IDT → ACEScg

two different cameras shoot the same scene; each Input Device Transform maps native RGB into a single shared ACES 2065-1 space

The Academy Colour Encoding System fixes one neutral working space (ACES 2065-1, AP0 primaries, D60) so VFX, grading, and delivery can share assets. The IDT is the camera-specific 3×3 matrix that converts that camera's native RGB into ACES 2065-1. A second fixed matrix (AP0 → AP1) takes ACES 2065-1 into ACEScg for working.

Three RGB patches stand in for a tiny scene. Toggle the camera — its IDT changes, the native RGB displayed changes, but the resulting ACES 2065-1 and ACEScg should be the same. That's what an IDT buys you.
